raw_result.description: Web development platform for building, deploying, and managing modern websites and applications, with a documented API for managing sites, deploys, forms, DNS, and related resources.
raw_result.auth_notes: Netlify's API docs document OAuth applications and personal access tokens for authentication.
raw_result.gating_reason: Netlify publishes self-serve pricing with a Free tier on its public pricing page, indicating developers can sign up and begin using the platform without sales approval.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API documented under api.netlify.com with resource endpoints for sites, deploys, forms, DNS, users, and related platform objects; the docs also reference an OpenAPI specification.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No first-party MCP server documentation was verified in the gathered official Netlify docs/pages.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: The gathered API docs indicate a general limit of 500 requests per minute, with stricter limits for deploy creation endpoints noted in the docs.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs describe REST endpoints under api.netlify.com/api/v1, document both OAuth and personal access token auth, and the pricing page is public with self-serve tiers including Free, suggesting low friction for an initial toolkit.
